content.full_text:
March 11, 1992


Ms. Elaine R. Murphy
Treasurer
Committee to Recall
293 Juanita Avenue
Pacifica, CA  94044

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-92-116

Dear Ms. Murphy:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1

                            QUESTIONS

     1.  Can committee funds be used for a victory party the night
of the election?

     2.  Can committee funds also be used for a "thank you" event
for the campaign workers and supporters of the recall effort?

                            CONCLUSIONS

     1.  The committee's campaign funds may be used for an
election victory celebration on the night of the election.

     2.  Since a "thank you" event for campaign workers and
supporters will be held shortly after the election and it is
essentially an election celebration, committee campaign funds may
be used to have a "thank you" event.



_________________________
     1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Sections
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-116
Page 2


FACTS

You are currently the treasurer of the Committee to Recall in
Pacifica, I.D. # 910744.

The committee would like to hold an event to thank campaign
workers and active supporters of the recall effort. This event
would be a picnic or pot luck dinner, or something akin to that.
The "thank you" event would take place before the end of March.

The committee would also like to put on a victory party the
night of the election. The special election will be held on
March 10, 1992.

ANALYSIS

Under Section 89512.5(b), "[a]ny expenditure by a committee
that confers a substantial personal benefit on any individual or
individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign
funds held by the committee, shall be directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose of the committee."

Section 89513 provided a list of specific activities for
which campaign funds may be used. Under Section 89513(f)(2),

"[a]n election victory celebration or similar
campaign event, or gifts with a total cumulative
value of less than two hundred fifty dollars ($250)
in a single year made to an individual employee, a
committee worker, or an employee of the elected
officer's agency, are considered to be directly
related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose."

Campaign funds used for an election victory celebration are
statutorily deemed to be directly related to a political,
legislative or governmental purpose. Therefore, the committee may
use campaign funds for a victory party the night of the election.

Turning to the "thank you" event, it will be held close in
time to the election, before the end of March (i.e., within three
weeks of the date of the election). Its purpose is to "thank"
campaign workers and supporters of the recall effort. Because of
these characteristics, the "thank you" event is in the nature of
an election victory celebration. Consequently, the event would
fall under the permissible use of campaign funds provision found
in section 89513(f)(2). As a result, it would be permissible in
this case to use campaign funds for a "thank you" event.

Our File No. A-92-116
Page 3


If you have any further questions, feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.2

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

SH:CMM:aa

Prepared by Chris Micheli, Legal Intern




2    Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
